implemented.summary: Added a deterministic delivered-status rule for observed complaint and lawsuit has_metric relations. The rule is scoped to entity subjects, metric_claim objects, same-source evidence, concrete numeric values, observed period anchors, complaint/lawsuit metric-name signals, count/complaints/lawsuits unit evidence or explicit missing-unit complaint/lawsuit names, and existing future/proposed/estimate rejection guards.

verification.details: Materialized 93092/98102 submissions; SQLite FTS quick_check: ok
Submissions 98102; canonical records 84040; wiki pages 7334; Issues 0

intentionally_unresolved: Thirteen complaint/lawsuit-shaped has_metric rows remain unknown because they lack an observed period anchor in metric payload/display fields.

The rule does not classify route or corridor complaint metrics, percentage/rate complaint metrics, missing-evidence rows, proposal/plan/estimate rows, or broad count metrics without complaint/lawsuit name evidence.
No canonical JSONL was hand-edited; canonical relations and SQLite were refreshed by deterministic materialization.
next_lanes: Continue relation assertion-status cleanup on metric_context.has_metric residuals with similarly narrow endpoint/unit/period guards.
Scout timeline_context.has_timeline_event and agency_role residuals for the next measurable deterministic rule.
Keep route/project duplicate identity checks periodic, but current high-confidence duplicate pressure is drained.
